title: Space Tourism Takes Off
subtitle: 普通人离太空旅行还有多远

paragraphs.en: Space tourism has transitioned from science fiction fantasy to emerging commercial reality. Private companies like SpaceX, Blue Origin, and Virgin Galactic have successfully launched paying customers beyond Earth's atmosphere. These pioneering flights herald a new era where space travel extends beyond trained astronauts.
paragraphs.zh: 太空旅游已经从科幻幻想转变为新兴的商业现实。SpaceX、蓝色起源和维珍银河等私营公司已经成功将付费客户送往地球大气层之外。这些开创性的飞行预示着一个新时代的到来，太空旅行将不再仅限于训练有素的宇航员。

paragraphs.en: Current space tourism options range from brief suborbital flights to extended stays aboard the International Space Station. Suborbital experiences lasting only minutes cost hundreds of thousands of dollars, while orbital missions can exceed fifty million. These prices ensure space remains accessible only to the ultra-wealthy for now.
paragraphs.zh: 目前的太空旅游选择从短暂的亚轨道飞行到在国际空间站的长期停留不等。仅持续几分钟的亚轨道体验需要花费数十万美元，而轨道任务可能超过五千万美元。这些价格确保了目前太空仅对超级富豪开放。

paragraphs.en: Critics question the ethics of billionaire space adventures while Earth faces pressing challenges. The carbon footprint of rocket launches raises environmental concerns, though companies claim technological improvements will reduce impacts. Some argue that space development ultimately benefits humanity through technological innovation and resources.
paragraphs.zh: 批评者质疑在地球面临紧迫挑战的情况下，亿万富翁太空冒险的伦理性。火箭发射的碳足迹引发了环境担忧，尽管公司声称技术改进将减少影响。一些人认为，太空开发最终通过技术创新和资源造福人类。

paragraphs.en: The space tourism industry anticipates dramatic cost reductions through reusable rocket technology and increased competition. Industry projections suggest that suborbital flights could eventually become comparable in price to luxury international travel. Such democratization would transform space from exclusive privilege to accessible adventure.
paragraphs.zh: 太空旅游行业预计，通过可重复使用的火箭技术和竞争加剧，成本将大幅降低。行业预测表明，亚轨道飞行最终可能在价格上与豪华国际旅行相当。这种民主化将把太空从专属特权转变为可及的冒险。

paragraphs.en: Beyond tourism, the commercialization of space may drive permanent human settlement beyond Earth. Hotels in orbit, research stations, and eventually lunar bases represent plausible developments within coming decades. Whether as tourists or pioneers, more humans will likely experience space firsthand than ever previously imagined.
paragraphs.zh: 除了旅游之外，太空商业化可能推动人类在地球以外的永久定居。轨道酒店、研究站，以及最终的月球基地，都是未来几十年内可能的发展。无论是作为游客还是开拓者，更多人类可能亲身体验太空，超出以往的想象。
